requirement.original: 学习dev系列commands对close -> commit/merge -> push 工作流的适配，将这个结尾工作流也加入到clean的适配。同时告诉我还有哪些command 也需要加收尾工作流的适配
requirement.clarified: Study how dev-series commands (dev.md, dev-command.md) adapt their ending workflow to the close→commit/merge→push chain. Add an equivalent (architecture-correct) ending-workflow adaptation to clean.md. Enumerate all other commands/*.md that also need ending-workflow adaptation.
requirement.what: Add /push next-step guidance to clean.md Step 17/18; enumerate other commands lacking ending-workflow guidance
requirement.why: clean.md and several other commands modify files and commit them but do not tell the user how to publish the result to the remote, creating a workflow gap relative to dev.md's explicit close→commit→push chain

included: Specification of what ending-workflow guidance to add to clean.md
Exhaustive enumeration of all commands/*.md with/without ending-workflow adaptation
Per-command tier classification (A: full chain, B: push-only, C: no adaptation needed)
excluded: Modifying how the cleaner subagent commits inline

success_criteria: clean.md ending-workflow adaptation is specified with correct architecture (no /close gate)
All 33 commands/*.md files are classified into tier A, B, or C with justification
Enumeration is exhaustive — no file-modifying command without /push guidance is missing
constraints: /close cannot be applied to /clean — it requires ticket+context+dev-report+qa-report+close-report under matching task-id
The clean.md adaptation must be /push guidance only, not a full dev-cycle chain
The cleaner subagent's inline commit behavior is unchanged
Only commands that modify files and lack any chain guidance qualify for Tier A or B

out_of_scope_observations.observation: test.md's test-executor subagent can apply fix scripts that modify files, but this is a secondary conditional path; primary output is reports. Ending-workflow adaptation is low-priority and conditional on fix mode being used.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: clean.md ends with 'Changes already committed' but does not guide the user to /push or to the close→commit→push chain
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: The dev-series commands (dev.md, dev-command.md) were designed with an explicit ending-workflow step that points to /close then /commit then /push. When clean.md was authored, this ending-workflow pattern was not propagated into it or into other file-modifying commands outside the dev-cycle architecture.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — architectural gap, not a regression
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: clean.md follows a different architecture (cleaner subagent commits inline, no dev-cycle artifact chain); the ending-workflow gap was left because the /close path is architecturally inapplicable and no simpler /push-only guidance was added instead
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Users completing a /clean run do not know the next step to publish changes to remote; they may either not push at all or run ad-hoc git commands without the guard protections
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Gap exists from initial clean.md authoring; not a regression
observations: commands/clean.md Step 17 Next Steps block (lines 585-591) contains 'Changes already committed' and checkpoint rollback but no /push (inferred: file reading)
commands/dev.md Step 15 lines 1056-1073 explicit /close → /commit → /push chain documented (inferred: file reading)
commands/push.md push-gate requires prior /commit token (inferred: file reading)
commands/close.md lines 159-165 preflight requires ticket+context+dev-report+qa-report+completion artifacts (inferred: file reading)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — no runtime browser observation applicable for command documentation gap)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Read commands/clean.md: Step 17 Next Steps block (lines 585-591) — no /push mention. Read commands/dev.md: Step 15 lines 1056-1073 — explicit /close chain. Read commands/close.md: lines 159-165 artifact preflight — /close requires full dev-cycle artifact chain that /clean does not produce.

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — not a regression; gap exists from initial authoring

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: 将这个结尾工作流也加入到clean的适配
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: clean.md Step 17/18 gains a next-step block: satisfied → /push; unsatisfied → checkpoint rollback. /close is NOT imposed.

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: 同时告诉我还有哪些command 也需要加收尾工作流的适配
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Concrete enumeration with per-command tier (A/B/C) and justification covering all 33 commands/*.md files.

component_chain.role: Reference pattern — shows the complete /update --temp → /close → /commit → /push ending workflow for dev-cycle commands
Target — Step 17 Next Steps block and Step 18 completion report; no /push guidance present
component_chain.evidence: Read commands/clean.md lines 559-692: Next Steps says 'Changes already committed', 'git diff HEAD~1', checkpoint rollback — no /push

component_chain.role: Architectural gate — normal path preflight requires ticket+context+dev-report+qa-report+completion artifacts; /clean produces none of these
Terminal step — /push publishes committed changes to remote; requires push-gate token from prior /commit
component_chain.evidence: Read commands/push.md: push-gate token required, human-runs push.sh script

component_chain.role: Gap candidate — dev-cycle command with weak ending-workflow (Offer git commit, no explicit /close chain)
component_chain.evidence: Read commands/dev-command.md lines 900-914: 'Offer git commit (if requested)' — no explicit /close or /commit or /push chain

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: This step MUST delegate ALL cleanup execution to the cleaner subagent via the Agent tool.
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Prevents orchestrator from directly executing cleanup; all file modifications go through cleaner subagent
Safety checkpoint before cleanup execution — enables rollback

setup.reason: non-UI -- doc-only; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: User reports dev-series commands adapt to close→commit/merge→push workflow but clean.md does not

measured.value: clean.md Step 17 Next Steps: '2. If satisfied: Changes already committed / 3. If not satisfied: git reset --hard <checkpoint_commit>' — no /push mention

expected.value: Ending-workflow next-step block equivalent to dev.md Step 15: satisfied → /push; unsatisfied → checkpoint rollback

evidence.gap: clean.md is missing a /push step after the cleaner's inline commit; user has no guidance on how to publish the cleanup to remote

all_occurrences: commands/quick-prototype.md:end (gap — no /commit or /push after HTML creation)

reference_source.description: Direct reading of commands/dev.md, commands/clean.md, commands/close.md, commands/push.md, commands/commit.md, commands/merge.md, commands/do.md, commands/dev-overnight.md, and all other commands/*.md files in this session

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts exist for this requirement

tier_A_full_devchain_needed.gap: Step 14 says 'Offer git commit (if requested)' — no explicit /close → /commit → /push chain reference
tier_A_full_devchain_needed.adaptation: Add /update --temp → suggest /close → /commit → /push chain, mirroring dev.md Step 15

tier_B_push_only_needed.gap: Step 17 Next Steps has no /push mention after inline cleaner commit
tier_B_push_only_needed.adaptation: Add next-step guidance: satisfied → /push; unsatisfied → checkpoint rollback

tier_B_push_only_needed.gap: Ends with 'Report changes made' — no /commit or /push mention after editing INDEX.md and CLAUDE.md auto-sections
tier_B_push_only_needed.adaptation: Add a note: if changes were made, they are uncommitted edits that can be reviewed with git diff, then committed and published with /push

tier_B_push_only_needed.gap: Ends after git commit with no /push mention
tier_B_push_only_needed.adaptation: Add a one-line note: 'After the commit, run /push to publish to remote'

tier_B_push_only_needed.gap: Creates HTML prototype files; no /commit or /push guidance
tier_B_push_only_needed.adaptation: Add a one-line note: 'To version-control: commit the file then /push'

file_contents.commands/clean.md:Step17-NextSteps: 2. If satisfied: Changes already committed / 3. If not satisfied: git reset --hard <checkpoint_commit>
file_contents.commands/dev.md:Step15-WorkflowUpdate: If complete → /update --temp → next command /close <task-id>; full chain: /close → /commit → /push

development_approach.strategy: Dev adds a 'Next Step' or 'Workflow Continuation' block to commands/clean.md Step 17/18. The block mirrors dev.md Step 15's complete-work branch but replaces /close with /push (clean has inline commits, no dev artifact chain). Dev also records the enumeration finding in a brief informational section for the user.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA verifies: (1) /push appears in clean.md Step 17 or Step 18 next-step block; (2) /close does NOT appear as a required step in clean.md; (3) the enumeration table is present and accurate; (4) the cleaner subagent inline commit behavior is unchanged

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex invocation blocked by pretool-tool-policy.py hook (BA agent write restriction prevents codex exec from writing output to /var/tmp/codex-outputs/). Also blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh on first attempt (prompt contained 'git reset --hard' as literal text). Self-review substituted.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — reviewed 5 adversarial questions: (1) /close inapplicability to clean confirmed correct; (2) quick-commit.md in-scope justified (it modifies/commits but no /push); (3) dev-command.md in-scope as dev-series command confirmed; (4) doc-sync.md and quick-prototype.md low-medium priority confirmed; (5) test.md correctly placed out-of-path as observation-only
